sanskrit: अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत्। इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम्॥
transliteration: abhisandhāya tu phalaṃ dambhārtham api caiva yat | ijyate bhārataśreṣṭha taṃ yajñaṃ viddhi rājasam ||
literal_meaning: Having in view the fruit, for the sake of ostentation, that which is performed as worship—know that sacrifice to be of the nature of passion, O best of the Bharatas.
contextual_meaning: Any ritual, project, or self-improvement effort that is secretly (or overtly) performed to gain applause, status, or a transactional reward is driven by the restless energy of rajas. It may look impressive, but it binds the doer to external validation and fleeting results.
psychological_state: Performative self-esteem—actions are hijacked by the ego’s need to prove worth, creating a cycle of short-lived highs and chronic dissatisfaction.
trigger_scenario: Posting a charity donation screenshot on social media mainly to harvest likes, then feeling hollow when the notifications stop.
anchor_text: Work done for applause chains you to others’ opinions.
